thesis: Leading enterprise cloud workflow platform combining durable 20%+ revenue growth with best-in-class profitability and an underappreciated AI monetization story. The Now Platform's deep enterprise embedment, land-and-expand dynamics, and native AI integration (Now Assist) position ServiceNow as the primary workflow automation platform for the AI era. Q1 2026 subscription revenue grew 22% YoY, FY2026 guidance was raised to $15.735B–$15.775B, and Now Assist target was raised to $1.5B. 35%+ FCF margins and a $5B buyback authorization support a premium valuation with 18–28% base-case upside.

drivers: AI monetization exceeds targets: Now Assist reaches $3B+ by FY2027 and drives multi-product attach rates above 6 per customer
Operating margins expand to 35%+ and FCF margins reach 40%, rewarded with 15x EV/Revenue multiple
Platform consolidation accelerates: enterprises reduce 5–7 vendors to 2–3, increasing NRR above 115%
Large deal momentum sustains 25%+ growth in $1M+ and $10M+ ACV contracts

risks: Macro IT budget cuts delay transformation projects and slow expansion into HRSD, CSM, and Creator
Microsoft and Salesforce bundling compresses mid-market pricing and slows new logo growth
Growth decelerates to 15% as revenue scales beyond $15B, compressing EV/Revenue multiple to 8–9x
AI monetization disappoints if enterprise AI adoption is slower than expected

base_case.assumptions: Revenue grows 20–21% through FY2026, FCF margin sustains 35%+, market assigns 12x EV/Revenue on best-in-class SaaS metrics and platform leadership

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 Earnings — confirms FY2026 subscription revenue and margin guidance
Knowledge annual user conference — new product announcements and Now Assist updates
Q3 2026 Earnings — first look at FY2027 guidance and Now Assist run-rate
AI agent general availability and monetization metrics
Strategic partnership updates (NVIDIA, Microsoft) and joint go-to-market expansion

key_risks: Premium valuation (~12x EV/Revenue) leaves limited room for execution missteps
Microsoft and Salesforce bundling threat in price-sensitive mid-market segments
Natural growth deceleration at $15B+ scale could compress multiples
AI monetization risk if enterprise AI deployment is slower than expected
